commit.message:
Cursor/development environment setup 3671 (#133)

* Add Cursor Cloud specific instructions for Linux development

- Document Linux-specific environment setup (GPU acceleration, node-pty rebuild, spawn-helper compilation)
- Document dev mode race condition workaround
- Note cr-sqlite and computer-use feature limitations on Linux
- Add tips for running Electron app and tests efficiently

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cr-sqlite sync API to gracefully degrade on Linux

The sync API methods (getDbVersion, exportChangesSince, applyChanges)
threw when cr-sqlite extension was unavailable, preventing project
initialization on platforms without the extension (Linux).

Changed to return safe no-op defaults (0, [], empty result) instead
of throwing, allowing the sync service to initialize harmlessly
without CRDT sync capability.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove accidentally committed .ade db backup files

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix onboarding to trigger on new machines with tracked .ade directory

Check for ade.db (gitignored, machine-local) instead of the .ade
directory (tracked in git with shared config). This ensures onboarding
runs when a project is cloned to a new machine that has the shared
scaffold but no local runtime state.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add env var fallback for GitHub, Linear, and AI API key credentials

Allows headless/CI/cloud environments to provide credentials via
environment variables when Electron's safeStorage encrypted store
is empty or unavailable.

- GitHub: GITHUB_TOKEN or ADE_GITHUB_TOKEN
- Linear: LINEAR_API_KEY or ADE_LINEAR_TOKEN
- AI providers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY,
  MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY,
  TOGETHER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY

The encrypted store remains the primary source. Env vars are only
checked as a fallback when no stored credential is found.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-complete onboarding when env var credentials are detected

When a project is opened for the first time on a new machine and
environment variables provide at least one credential (GitHub token,
AI API key, or Linear token), mark onboarding as complete so the
wizard is skipped. This lets cloud agents and CI environments that
set credentials via env vars go straight to the Work view.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: expand AI provider env var check in onboarding auto-complete to cover all 9 supported providers

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/arul28/ADE/sessions/e1b5e26f-5a60-433c-8c20-80be8492a456

Co-authored-by: arul28 <31745423+arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Shadow Lift preset using power-curve gamma algorithm

Implements the shadow lift algorithm with gamma=1.5 (exponent≈0.667).
The power curve raises dark tones more than bright tones while keeping
pure white anchored. Max deviation from identity is ~9,800 at i≈75,
well within the 32,000 driver safety limit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prove znz_p_field: Z/pZ forms a field for prime p

- Add znz_field_inv_val: epsilon-based inverse for Z/pZ
- Add znz_field_inv_spec: inverse correctness lemma
- Add znz_p_ring: Ring instance for Z/pZ
- Add znz_p_field: Field instance for Z/pZ (prime p)
- Update progress/field_progress.md: mark all complete
- Update proof_knowledge_base.md: add entries for new definitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

体 (Field) と環 (Ring) の定義を追加

- Record Ring: 加法可換群 + 乗法モノイド + 分配法則の 11 公理
- Ring 基本補題: ring_add_zero_r, ring_add_neg_r, ring_add_cancel_l,
  ring_mul_zero_l/r, ring_neg_neg, ring_neg_mul_l/r
- Record Field: Ring への強制型変換 + field_inv + field_mul_comm +
  field_inv_l + field_one_ne_zero
#- Field 基本
: field_inv_r, field_inv_nonzero, field_no_zero_divisors,
  field_mul_cancel_l, field_div
- 補助補題: znz_prime_nonzero_coprime (素p に対し gcd(a mod p, p) = 1)
- znz_p_field: Admitted (field_inv の型設計が要検討)
- progress/field_plan.md, progress/field_progress.md を
- proof_knowledge_base.md に Ring/Field 関連の知識を追記

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prove count_multiples_in_range and prime_pow_coprime_distinct

Both Admitted lemmas in integer.v are now fully proven, making
euler_phi_three_prime_powers Admitted-free.

New sub-lemmas added before count_multiples_in_range:
- mod_add_mul_small: (p*m+r) mod p = r for r < p
- filter_false_forall: Forall (f x = false) l -> filter f l = []
- filter_window_single_multiple: filter (k mod p =? 0) (seq (p*m) p) = [p*m]

Proofs:
- count_multiples_in_range: induction on m, seq_app + filter_app + above
- prime_pow_coprime_distinct: 3-step via prime_rel_prime/Zis_gcd_gcd,
  Z.coprime_pow_l/r, Z.gcd_greatest/divide_1_r

Updated progress/euler_phi_progress.md and proof_knowledge_base.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add euler_phi definition and main theorem euler_phi_three_prime_powers

- euler_phi: Euler's totient function (counts k in {0,...,n-1} with gcd(k,n)=1)
- group_order_unique, group_order_iso, group_order_product: GroupOrder algebraic properties
- euler_phi_group_order: phi(n) = |units(Z/nZ)| via bijection
- znz_units_decomp2: 2-var CRT isomorphism (Z/pqZ)* ≅ (Z/pZ)* × (Z/qZ)*
- euler_phi_mul: multiplicativity phi(p*q) = phi(p)*phi(q) when gcd(p,q)=1
- prime_pow_coprime_iff: gcd(k,p^e)=1 ↔ k mod p ≠ 0 for prime p
- euler_phi_prime_pow: phi(p^e) = p^(e-1)*(p-1) for prime p, e≥1 (depends on Admitted count lemma)
- nat_gcd_mul_coprime: gcd(a,c)=1 ∧ gcd(b,c)=1 → gcd(a*b,c)=1 (Nat level, via Gauss)
- euler_phi_three_prime_powers: main theorem phi(p^e*q^f*r^g) = p^(e-1)(p-1)*q^(f-1)(q-1)*r^(g-1)(r-1)
  (depends on Admitted: count_multiples_in_range, prime_pow_coprime_distinct)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
types: re-record range bound spans with inferred element type (#634)

For-loop range literals like `0..10` are synthesized with a fresh TypeVar
for the element type so the loop body can constrain it (e.g. from an
`i32` accumulator).  The *range* TypeVar was already unified correctly,
but the *bound literal spans* (the `0` and `10`) were permanently
recorded as `I64` by the initial `synthesize` call.

Because codegen uses `resolvedTypeOf(span)` in `generateLiteral` to
choose the MLIR constant width, this caused two issues:
1. Bounds were always emitted as i64 constants even when the range was
   inferred to be `Range<i32>`, producing unnecessarily wide IR.
2. A bound that doesn't fit in the inferred type (e.g. `0..300` with
   `i8` constraint) would silently generate a truncated constant —
   e.g. 300 → 44 — causing wrong iteration counts at runtime.

Fix:
- Add `deferred_range_bounds: Vec<(Span, TypeVar, Option<i64>)>` to
  Checker, populated in `check_binary_op` when both bounds are coercible
  integer literals.
- Add `apply_deferred_range_bound_types` which runs after
  `default_unconstrained_range_types` in `check_program`.  It resolves
  each `TypeVar` to its concrete type, validates the literal fits (emitting
  an error if not), and re-records the bound span with the correct type.
- Add a `check_against` arm for `(Expr::Binary{Range|RangeInclusive},
  Range<T>)` so that range literals in typed positions (function args,
  `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`) check the bounds directly against `T`.

Tests:
- `for_range_infers_i32_from_body_usage`: verifies bound spans are
  re-recorded as I32 (not I64) when body constrains the induction variable.
- `range_literal_check_against_range_i32`: range literal coerces to a
  `Range<i32>` parameter without errors.
- `for_range_unconstrained_defaults_to_i64`: unconstrained range still
  defaults to i64 (no regression).
- `for_range_bound_overflow_errors`: `0..300` with an i8 constraint now
  correctly errors instead of silently truncating.
- `range_literal_assigned_to_range_i32`: `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use {{flutter_js}} and {{flutter_build_config}} template tokens

Flutter 3.22+ requires these tokens for web initialization. The old
<script src=flutter.js> approach does not set _flutter.buildConfig,
causing 'FlutterLoader.load requires _flutter.buildConfig' error.
Now uses onEntrypointLoaded callback (supported with buildConfig) to
dismiss the loading splash after the app runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: loading screen stuck - use load() promise instead of callback

The new FlutterLoader.load() API auto-runs the app and returns a
promise; it does not support the onEntrypointLoaded callback from the
old deprecated loadEntrypoint API. Use .then() on the promise to
dismiss the loading splash after Flutter is ready.

commit.message:
fix(types): enforce actor boundary Send (#609)

* fix(types): enforce actor boundary Send

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(types): Actor<T> handle is always Send/Sync at spawn-capture boundary

The Send check for spawned lambda actor captures did not recognise the
built-in Actor<T> type (returned by 'spawn (x: T) => { ... }') as Send.
Only user-defined actors in self.actors were tested; Actor<T> fell
through to the field-based check and was incorrectly rejected.

This broke the pipeline pattern where one lambda actor captures another:

    let printer = spawn (x: int) => { println(x); };
    let doubler = spawn (x: int) => { printer.send(x * 2); };
                                     ^^^^^^^ Actor<int> capture

Add an explicit arm alongside ActorRef that marks Actor<T> as
Send + Sync + Clone + Debug. Add a regression test.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(hew-cli): pass drop_funcs to serialize_to_msgpack in compile_from_source_checked (#633)

* fix(types): mark implicit regex import used on RegexLiteral synthesis

The CLI injects a synthetic `import std::text::regex` when regex literals
are detected in source.  The type checker stores the import's short name
(`regex`) in `import_spans` but, when synthesising the `regex.Pattern`
type for a `RegexLiteral` expression, never inserted `regex` into
`used_modules`.  This caused the end-of-program unused-import sweep to
emit a false-positive "unused import: regex" warning for every file that
contained regex literals (e.g. examples/regex_demo.hew).

Fix: in the `Expr::RegexLiteral` arm of `synthesize_inner`, call
`self.used_modules.borrow_mut().insert("regex")` before returning the
`Ty::Named { name: "regex.Pattern" }` type, mirroring what the named-
module-call path already does for other stdlib modules.

Regression test added in `hew-types/tests/e2e_typecheck.rs`: compiles a
minimal program with an explicit `import std::text::regex` and a regex
literal, then asserts no `UnusedImport` warning is present.

* refactor(hew-observe): extract TraceEvent::is_actionable() predicate

The four-way event-type check ("send" | "spawn" | "crash" | "stop") was
duplicated verbatim in both app.rs (trace navigation scroll) and ui.rs
(timeline rendering).  Extract it as a method on TraceEvent in client.rs —
the type that owns the field — so there is a single source of truth.

Zero behaviour change: same four strings, same filter semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(hew-cli): pass drop_funcs to serialize_to_msgpack in compile_from_source_checked

The compile_from_source_checked function (used by hew eval / REPL path)
called serialize_to_msgpack with 6 arguments, omitting drop_funcs between
handle_type_repr and source_path. The function signature requires 7 args:

  serialize_to_msgpack(program, expr_types, handle_types,
                       handle_type_repr, drop_funcs,  ← missing
                       source_path, line_map)

The first call site (compile(), ~line 701) was already correct.
Only compile_from_source_checked (~line 805) was broken.

Fix: thread meta.drop_funcs into the call, restoring drop-function
metadata parity between the two compile paths.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtime/wasm): restore actor arena lifecycle parity (#617)

* fix(runtime/wasm): install and reset actor arena during WASM activation

The WASM scheduler's activate_actor_wasm did not call set_current_arena
before dispatch or hew_arena_reset after it, leaving a parity gap with
the native scheduler.

Changes:
- Add arena_install / arena_reset shim fns with cfg guards:
  non-WASM calls real crate::arena fns; wasm32 uses no-op stubs (WASM-TODO)
- In activate_actor_wasm:
  - Replace PREV_ARENA=null with PREV_ARENA=arena_install(a.arena) so the
    actor's arena is installed before dispatch and the prior arena is saved
  - Cache actor_arena before the dispatch loop (avoids dangling read if
    terminate_fn frees the actor)
  - After the loop: let _ = arena_install(PREV_ARENA) to restore the
    previous arena (discard return explicit), then arena_reset(actor_arena)
    with a comment explaining unconditional reset is safe on WASM because
    there is no siglongjmp crash path that could issue a competing reset
- reset_globals: clear thread-local current arena for test isolation
- Add four focused tests: install+clear, cursor reset, reentrant restore,
  null-arena baseline

Pre-existing flaky failures (unrelated to this change):
  hew_node::tests::two_node_remote_* — flaky under concurrent test load on main

Residual gaps out of scope: wasm32 arena allocator, terminate_fn,
sys_len, try_send, mailbox-close.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fixup! fix(runtime/wasm): install and reset actor arena during WASM activation

Repair 12 Clippy errors introduced by the arena lifecycle tests (CI job
69951950131, PR #617):

- items_after_statements (6): hoist static ARENA_DURING_DISPATCH,
  capture_arena_dispatch, alloc_in_dispatch, static OUTER_POST_DISPATCH,
  outer_dispatch, and queue_wasm_message_static to the top of their
  respective test function bodies, before any let-statements.

- undocumented_unsafe_blocks (4): add // SAFETY: comments to the p2-alloc
  block in arena_is_reset_after_activation, and to the three bare unsafe
  blocks inside outer_dispatch and queue_wasm_message_static.

- redundant/unnecessary raw-pointer cast (2, same line): remove the
  redundant 'as *mut *mut HewActor' cast in the inner_ptr state
  assignment; &raw mut inner_ptr is already *mut *mut HewActor.

No behavior changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fixup! fix(runtime/wasm): install and reset actor arena during WASM activation

cargo fmt: align comment in queue_wasm_message_static to rustfmt output.
The // SAFETY comment before the wake_wasm_actor call was indented at the
function-body level (12 spaces); rustfmt places it at the continuation
indent of the preceding unsafe block expression (24 spaces). One-line
formatting-only change; no behavior or logic change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: 既約剰余類群の分解定理 znz_units_decomp を証明

pairwise coprime な p, q, r に対して:
  (Z/pqrZ)^* ≅ (Z/pZ)^* × (Z/qZ)^* × (Z/rZ)^*

#
: 1775132645:1;rocq compile integer.8個）:
- znz_units_gcd_dvd: gcd(a, m·n)=1 → gcd(a, m)=1
- znz_units_gcd_mul: gcd(a,m)=1 ∧ gcd(a,n)=1 → gcd(a,m·n)=1
- znz_units_coprime_mod_l/m/r: gcd(a,pqr)=1 → gcd(a mod p, p)=1 等
- znz_units_gcd_mul3: 3因子版 gcd 合成
- znz_units_decomp_mul_mod: 乗算の mod 分配補題
- Theorem znz_units_decomp: 主定理

: 1775132645:1;rocq compile integer.v:
- 写像: phi([a]) = (([a mod p], [a mod q]), [a mod r])
- 準同型性: znz_mod_mod_l/m/r + Z.mul_mod
- 単射性: crt_unique_3
- 全射性: crt_exists_3 + znz_units_gcd_mul3

commit.message:
docs: znz_units_group に詳細な日本語コメントを追加

- 定義スタイル（refine + Proof/Defined）の説明
- シグマ型と exist の役割を解説
- proj1_sig/proj2_sig/conj/proj1/proj2 の型と用途を記述
- epsilon を使う理由（存在命題から値を取り出す古典論理）を説明
- op フィールドの補題 (Z.mod_pos_bound, znz_gcd_mod_eq,
  znz_gcd_mul_coprime, eq_trans) の役割をコメント
- 群公理証明の補題 (sig_eq, Zmult_mod_idemp_l/r, Z.mul_1_l/r,
  Z.mod_small, Z.mod_add) の役割をコメント

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

echo  (Z/nZ)* の定義を追加

Z/nZ のうち n と互いに素な元の全体 (Z/nZ)* を乗算に関する群として
integer.v に定義する。

#に定義する。
:
- znz_gcd_one: Z.gcd 1 (Z.of_nat n) = 1
- znz_gcd_mod_eq: gcd(a mod n, n) = gcd(a, n)
- znz_gcd_mul_coprime: gcd(a,n)=1 and gcd(b,n)=1 => gcd(a*b,n)=1
- znz_coprime_bezout_inv: 乗法逆元の存在 (linear_diophantine 経由)
- znz_units_inv_val: epsilon による逆元値の定義
- znz_units_inv_prop: 逆元の性質
- znz_units_group: 既約剰余類群 (Z/nZ)* の Group インスタンス

: {x : Z | 0 <= x < Z.of_nat n /\ Z.gcd x (Z.of_nat n) = 1}
: 1775132645:1;rocq integer.v compile: [a] * [b] = [a*b mod n], 単位元: [1], 逆元: epsilon で構成

progress/znz_units_plan.md, progress/znz_units_progress.md を追加。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add znz_decomp: Z/(pqr)Z iso Z/pZ x Z/qZ x Z/rZ

p, q, r pairwise coprime のとき Z/(pqr)Z が Z/pZ x Z/qZ x Z/rZ
: 1775132645:1;rocq compile integer.v

:
- znz_dvd_mul3_l/m/r: p, q, r が p*q*r を割る補助補題
- znz_mod_mod_l/m/r: (a mod (p*q*r)) mod p = a mod p
- Theorem znz_decomp: 主定理

echo phi([a]) = (([a mod p], [a mod q]), [a mod r]) で
 (crt_unique_3)・全射性 (crt_exists_3) を証明。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix integer.v compilation errors

1. Line 2120: Replace failing 'nia' with Z.divide_1_r_nonneg
   - Proof: 'Z.gcd(p*q, r) | 1' and '0 <= Z.gcd' → 'Z.gcd = 1'
   - nia could not find a Positivstellensatz witness for this fact

2. Lines 2122-2124: Replace missing Nat2Z.inj_gcd (not in stdlib)
   - Add helper lemma nat_divide_to_Z_of_nat (Nat.divide → Z.divide)
   - Derive Nat.gcd = 1 from Z.gcd = 1 via Z.gcd_greatest + Z.divide_1_r_nonneg

3. Line 2181: Fix coprime_divide_mul_3 - (Z.of_nat(p*q)|a) type mismatch
   - Hpq_div had type (Z.of_nat p * Z.of_nat q | a), not (Z.of_nat(p*q) | a)
   - Add explicit rewrite Nat2Z.inj_mul instead of relying on assumption

4. Lines 2185-2188: Simplify coprime_divide_mul_3 ending
   - Remove confused replace tactic; just rewrite in Hmul and exact

5. Lines 2212-2215: Fix exists! destructure patterns in crt_exists_3
   - chinese_remainder returns exists! (unique existence) not exists
   - Add missing uniqueness component '_' in destruct patterns

6. Line 2214: Fix type mismatch in crt_exists_3
   - Htbound: Z.of_nat p * Z.of_nat q vs Z.of_nat (p * q)
   - Add Htbound' assertion with rewrite Nat2Z.inj_mul

7. Lines 2226-2243: Fix 'with (m := ...)' in Z_scope causing nat/Z confusion
   - Remove explicit 'with' clauses; let Coq infer from goal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: cover drop-column operational-error fallback

Add explicit regression for the branch where SQLite reports DROP COLUMN support but execution fails with OperationalError, ensuring migration falls back to table rebuild and reconciliation remains clean.

- pairwise_coprime3_gcd_pq_r: nia タクティク失敗を Z.divide_1_r +
: 1775132645:1;Rocq Nat2Z.inj_gcd を
  Z.of_nat (Nat.gcd ...) への直接帰着方式に変更。
- coprime_divide_mul_3: Z.of_nat p * Z.of_nat q から Z.of_nat (p*q) への
  型変換が欠落していたため Hmul_pq 中間補題を追加。
- crt_exists_3: chinese_remainder が exists! を返すため destruct パターンに
Htbound' 変換ステップを追加。  uniqueness の _ が必要だっ
- crt_exists_3: apply cong_of_mod with (m := p * q) での nat/Z 型不整合を
  exact (cong_of_mod (p * q) n t ...) の

rocq compile integer.v がエラー 0 で成功することを確認済み。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-generate Terraform provider response field mapping and documentation (#29)

* feat: add auto-generated response field mapping to terraform provider

- Add ResponseFields to OperationDef in spec layer
- Add configurable FieldResolveOpts to body.go for request vs response skip lists
- Response fields use more permissive skip lists (include created_on, updated_on, etc.)
- Add ResolveResponseRef to extract response schema from OpenAPI responses
- Handle paginated responses by extracting item schema from values array
- Add GetNested helper to handlers for extracting nested response values
- Update resource Schema() to expose response fields as Computed attributes
- Body fields that overlap with response fields become Optional+Computed
- Update resource dispatch() to extract and set response field values
- Update datasource Schema() and Read() to support response fields
- Regenerate all .gen.go files with response fields

* feat: update docs generator with response field documentation and fix .gitignore

- Add BodyFields, ResponseFields, OverlapFields to GroupData in gen_tfdocs
- Derive fields from registered ResourceGroups' CRUD operations
- Update resource doc template to show Optional body fields, Optional+Computed overlap fields, and Computed response fields
- Update data source doc template to show all computed response fields
- Update provider overview doc to explain the new attribute pattern
- Add gen_terraform to .gitignore and remove accidentally committed binary

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
fix: onboarding treats standby devices as logically connected

Devices in idle-standby were intentionally disconnected by the bridge
to save speaker battery.  The onboarding checks should not regress
when all devices are in standby — bluetooth_connected and
sink_verification now count standby devices as connected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
